text: Code refactoring is the process of improving the internal structure of existing code without changing its external behavior. The main goal is to make the code cleaner, more readable, and easier to maintain. For example, you might rename unclear variables, break long functions into smaller ones, or remove duplicate code. Although the program's functionality stays the same, the quality improves. This helps reduce technical debt — the hidden cost of poorly written code that can slow down future development. Regular refactoring makes software more flexible and saves time in the long run, as developers can understand and modify the code with less effort.
